manual_entries.title: Minimum Flow Bypass Valve - Transition Setpoint
manual_entries.summary: During stage changes with one boiler enabled and another disabled, temporarily set minimum flow setpoint to include both boilers' HW-MinFlowX.

structured_payload.title: Minimum Flow Bypass Valve - Transition Setpoint
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Plants that stage a boiler on while staging another off
structured_payload.summary: During stage changes with one boiler enabled and another disabled, temporarily set minimum flow setpoint to include both boilers' HW-MinFlowX.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Stage change requiring one boiler to be enabled while another is disabled.
structured_payload.required_behavior: HW-MinFlowSP temporarily includes HW-MinFlowX of both boilers prior to starting the newly enabled boiler.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: During stage changes that require one boiler to be enabled while another is disabled, the minimum flow setpoint, HW-MinFlowSP shall temporarily change to include the HW-MinFlowX of both the boiler to be enabled and the boiler to be disabled prior to starting the newly enabled boiler.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Calculate the minimum flow setpoint, HW -MinFlowSP as MinFlowRatio multiplied by the
sum of HW -DesFlowX for the operating boilers.
If the boilers have different minimum flow to design flow ratios, just maintaining the sum of the minimum flows will
not satisfy the boiler(s) with the highest relative minimum flows. Note that this also requires that boilers be
balanced to distribute flow pr oportional to their design flow.
Retain the following section  for plants that stage a boiler on while staging another off during any stage
change.

boiler to be enabled and the boiler to be disabled prior to starting the newly enabled boiler.
